arguments.content:
"""Markdown report generator from JSON data.

This module provides utilities for converting structured JSON data
into formatted Markdown reports with tables, headers, and sections.

Meta:
    created_at: 2026-04-16T07:36:00-04:00
    author: assistant
    status: active
"""

import json
from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional
from datetime import datetime


def generate_report(data_dict: Dict[str, Any], output_path: str) -> None:
    """Generate a Markdown report from JSON data and save to file.
    
    Args:
        data_dict: Dictionary containing report data with sections.
        output_path: Path where the Markdown file will be saved.
        
    Returns:
        None
        
    Example:
        >>> data = {
        ...     "title": "Q1 Performance Report",
        ...     "sections": [
        ...         {"heading": "Overview", "content": "Summary text..."},
        ...         {"heading": "Metrics", "table": [["Metric", "Value"], ["Uptime", "99.9%"]]}
        ...     ]
        ... }
        >>> generate_report(data, "report.md")
    """
    lines = []
    
    # Title
    if "title" in data_dict:
        lines.append(f"# {data_dict['title']}\n")
    
    # Generation timestamp
    lines.append(f"*Generated: {datetime.now().isoformat()}*\n")
    
    # Process sections
    sections = data_dict.get("sections", [])
    for section in sections:
        # Section heading
        if "heading" in section:
            lines.append(f"## {section['heading']}\n")
        
        # Paragraph content
        if "content" in section:
            lines.append(f"{section['content']}\n")
        
        # Table
        if "table" in section:
            table = section["table"]
            if table and len(table) > 0:
                # Header row
                header = table[0]
                lines.append("| " + " | ".join(str(cell) for cell in header) + " |")
                # Separator
                lines.append("|" + "|".join(" --- " for _ in header) + "|")
                # Data rows
                for row in table[1:]:
                    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(str(cell) for cell in row) + " |")
                lines.append("")
        
        # List
        if "list" in section:
            for item in section["list"]:
                lines.append(f"- {item}")
            lines.append("")
        
        # Code block
        if "code" in section:
            lang = section.get("code_language", "")
            lines.append(f"```{lang}")
            lines.append(section["code"])
            lines.append("```\n")
    
    # Metadata section if present
    if "metadata" in data_dict:
        lines.append("---\n")
        lines.append("## Metadata\n")
        for key, value in data_dict["metadata"].items():
            lines.append(f"- **{key}**: {value}")
        lines.append("")
    
    # Write to file
    with open(output_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write("\n".join(lines))


def generate_summary_table(data_list: List[Dict[str, Any]], columns: List[str]) -> str:
    """Generate a Markdown table from a list of dictionaries.
    
    Args:
        data_list: List of dictionaries containing data rows.
        columns: List of column names to include.
        
    Returns:
        Markdown formatted table string.
    """
    if not data_list or not columns:
        return ""
    
    lines = []
    # Header
    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(columns) + " |")
    lines.append("|" + "|".join(" --- " for _ in columns) + "|")
    
    # Rows
    for item in data_list:
        row = [str(item.get(col, "")) for col in columns]
        lines.append("| " + " | ".join(row) + " |")
    
    return "\n".join(lines)


def generate_bullet_list(items: List[str], numbered: bool = False) -> str:
    """Generate a Markdown list from items.
    
    Args:
        items: List of string items.
        numbered: If True, generate numbered list; otherwise bullet list.
        
    Returns:
        Markdown formatted list string.
    """
    lines = []
    for i, item in enumerate(items, 1):
        prefix = f"{i}." if numbered else "-"
        lines.append(f"{prefix} {item}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Self-test with sample data
    sample_data = {
        "title": "Test Report",
        "sections": [
            {
                "heading": "Summary",
                "content": "This is a test report generated from JSON data."
            },
            {
                "heading": "Metrics",
                "table": [
                    ["Item", "Value", "Status"],
                    ["Task A", "100%", "Complete"],
                    ["Task B", "75%", "In Progress"]
                ]
            },
            {
                "heading": "Action Items",
                "list": ["Review report", "Submit feedback", "Archive data"]
            }
        ],
        "metadata": {
            "author": "assistant",
            "version": "1.0.0"
        }
    }
    
    test_path = "day24/test_report.md"
    generate_report(sample_data, test_path)
    print(f"Test report generated: {test_path}")
    
    # Test table generation
    test_data = [
        {"name": "Alice", "role": "Dev", "score": 95},
        {"name": "Bob", "role": "QA", "score": 88}
    ]
    table_md = generate_summary_table(test_data, ["name", "role", "score"])
    print("\nGenerated table:")
    print(table_md)
    
    print("\nAll tests passed!")
